channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: Zimbabwe billionaire Kudakwashe Tagwirei commits $1 million to evacuate citizens from South Africa
item.description: Zimbabwe billionaire Kudakwashe Tagwirei commits $1 million to evacuate citizens from South Africa — via Business Insider Africa #Zimbabwe #Health #ZimbabweHealth #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Business Insider Africa

item.title: The Great Train Heist: How a 20-Man Robbery Gang Stripped NRZ of US$10,000 in Minutes
item.description: The National Railways of Zimbabwe (NRZ) has been hit by a massive and daring raid, where a 20-member gang attacked a feeder station and stole US$10,000 worth of critical parts. This was not a random act of theft; it was a professional and well-coordinated operation. The attack happened on the night of 23 June 2026 [...] The post The Great Train Heist: How a 20-Man Robbery Gang Stripped NRZ of US$10,000 in Minutes first appeared on My Zimbabwe News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: My Zimbabwe News

item.title: Tungwarara reignites Zanu-PF power battle @bulawayo24 @ZANUPF_Official
item.description: Zanu-PF central committee member and businessman Paul Tungwarara has declared a return to his empowerment programmes in Manicaland, reigniting his rivalry with fellow businessman Kudakwashe Tagwirei a... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: Fear grips migrants ahead of SA protests @bulawayo24
item.description: FEAR is mounting among migrants in South Africa ahead of planned anti-foreigner protests, with rights organisations warning of possible violence as tensions over unemployment, inequality and undocumen... #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: CAPS United parts ways with Magalane, eyes Arubi @bulawayo24
item.description: CAPS United have reportedly parted ways with goalkeeper Wallace Magalane amid allegations of match-fixing, although the club is yet to officially confirm the reasons behind his departure.Magalane’s ex... #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: Police hunt suspect after Beitbridge murder near night club @bulawayo24 @PoliceZimbabwe
item.description: THE Zimbabwe Republic Police (ZRP) has appealed to members of the public for information that could lead to the identification and arrest of a suspect linked to the murder of a 26-year-old man at Limp... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: Court clears doctor in ZACC obstruction case @bulawayo24
item.description: CHINHOYI resident magistrate Nyasha Marufu has acquitted Makonde district medical officer Gift Masoja of defeating or obstructing the course of justice after ruling that the State failed to prove its... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: Zimbabwe launches first Big Brother-style reality show @bulawayo24
item.description: ZIMBABWE’S first home-grown Big Brother-style reality television show, *Brother In House Zimbabwe*, officially premiered in Harare on Sunday night, with 35 contestants entering a purpose-built house t... #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: Bus conductor arrested over viral passenger assault video @bulawayo24
item.description: A bus conductor operating on the Harare-Makaha route has been arrested in connection with the alleged assault of two passengers in an incident captured on video that has since gone viral on social med... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: ‘I Will Beat You Up’: Prophet Makandiwa Divides Zimbabweans With Sermon On Tithing
item.description: Makandiwa Tells Non-Tithers To Leave His Church In Viral Sermon A Sunday sermon by United Family International Church leader Emmanuel Makandiwa has set Zimbabwe ablaze after he used explosive, hyperbolic language to confront members who dodge tithing. The Prophet told his congreg #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Iharare News

item.title: The Eswatini Connection: Why King Mswati really gave Wicknell Chivayo a Diplomatic Passport @wicknellchivayo
item.description: In the gilded halls of the Lozitha Palace, amidst the heavy scent of tradition and the quiet rustle of royal protocol, a scene unfolded that has left regional observers and legal experts questioning the very definition of diplomacy. King Mswati III, the last absolute monarch of Africa, stood beside a man whose reputation precedes him [...] The post The Eswatini Connection: Why King Mswati really gave Wicknell Chivayo a Diplomatic Passport first appeared on My Zimbabwe News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: My Zimbabwe News

item.title: SHOCKING VIRAL VIDEO: Brutal Bolt Cutter Bus Crew Thoroughly Beat Up Schoolboys, Rob Them of their US$20 Bus Fare
item.description: Bus Crew Brutality: A Growing Concern on Zimbabwe’s Roads Harare – A disturbing incident involving a Bolt Cutter Bus crew brutally assaulting school students has ignited public outrage and cast a harsh spotlight on the escalating issue of violence perpetrated by bus and kombi crews against passengers across Zimbabwe. The incident, captured in a viral [...] The post SHOCKING VIRAL VIDEO: Brutal Bolt Cutter Bus Crew Thoroughly Beat Up Schoolboys, Rob Them of their US$20 Bus Fare first appeared on My Zimbabwe News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: My Zimbabwe News

item.title: 30 June Deadline: 26,000 Zimbabweans Flee South Africa as Angry Locals Vow to Brutally Attack All Undocumented Foreigners
item.description: Johannesburg – As the ominous 30 June deadline looms, South Africa finds itself at a critical juncture, grappling with escalating tensions surrounding immigration. A mass exodus of Zimbabwean nationals is underway, with over 26,000 individuals having already returned home, many fleeing a climate of fear and hostility fuelled by anti-immigrant sentiment and threats of violence [...] The post 30 June Deadline: 26,000 Zimbabweans Flee South Africa as Angry Locals Vow to Brutally Attack All Undocumented Foreigners first appeared on My Zimbabwe News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: My Zimbabwe News

item.title: Thousands seek way out as South Africa braces for anti-immigrant protests
item.description: ‘You never know what people are planning to do to you, it’s not wise to wait and see what will happen’ The post Thousands seek way out as South Africa braces for anti-immigrant protests appeared first on Zimbabwe News Now. (via Zimbabwean News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Zimbabwean News

item.title: South Africa tightens security, urges calm as June 30 anti-immigration protests approach
item.description: By Xinhua SOUTH African authorities have tightened security measures and intensified public warnings ahead of planned anti-immigration demonstrations scheduled for June 30, amid growing tensions over undocumented migration and concerns about potential unrest. The March and March movement and several anti-immigration groups on Wednesday reiterated their demand that undocumented foreign nationals leave South Africa by [...] The post South Africa tightens security, urges calm as June 30 anti-immigration protests approach appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Scottland FC eyeing Mamelodi Sundowns’ Shalulile in blockbuster transfer bid
item.description: ZIMBABWE Premier Soccer League title holders Scottland FC are said to be chasing Mamelodi Sundowns marksman Peter Shalulile as they bolster their ranks for next season’s Confederation of African Football (CAF) campaign. According to popular publication FarPost, the ambitious Harare outfit has earmarked the Namibian forward as a priority signing and has already tabled a [...] The post Scottland FC eyeing Mamelodi Sundowns’ Shalulile in blockbuster transfer bid appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Quiet Money, Deep Roots: How the Oak Foundation Backs Zimbabwe’s Communities
item.description: The Geneva-based Oak Foundation has funded Zimbabwean causes since 1983. In 2025 its Zimbabwe programme made USD 7.9 million in grants, backing palliative care, landmine clearance, regenerative farming and care for children who have lost parents. #Zimbabwe #Policy #ZimbabwePolicy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Tapiwa Hillary Phillipo
